zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen於1150年4月30日出生在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福蓋爾德時，他的父親Heinrich I. von Geldern32歲，母親Agnes von Arnstein31歲。他和Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern至少有4個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1207年10月22日在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德享年57歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 韋塞爾坎-林特福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen于1150年4月30日出生在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福蓋爾德时，他的父亲Heinrich I. von Geldern32岁，母亲Agnes von Arnstein31岁。他和Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern至少有4个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1207年10月22日在荷兰蓋爾德藍德享年57岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 韋塞爾坎-林特福特。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Geldern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): in northern England usually a variant of Geldard, an occupational name from Middle English gelde ‘sterile, barren’, used as a noun to denote ‘gelded cattle’, + Old English hierde, hence ‘herdman who tends the gelded cattle’.
English: alternatively, perhaps an occupational name from Middle English gelder(e) ‘gelder, castrator (of livestock)’.
English: variant of   Gilder  .

variants.name: Gilder
meanings: English (Essex and Middlesex): occupational name for a goldsmith or gilder, from Middle English gilder, gelder, guldar, a derivative of Old English gyldan ‘to gild’.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: Zutphen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Sutphin  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Sutphin
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Van Zutphen: habitational name for someone from Zutphen in the province of Gelderland. Compare Sutfin and Sutphen .

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen was born on 30 April 1150, in Geldern, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, his father, Heinrich I. von Geldern, was 32 and his mother, Agnes von Arnstein, was 31. He had at least 4 sons and 4 daughters with Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. He died on 22 October 1207, in Zutphen, Gelderland, Netherlands, at the age of 57, and was buried in Kamp-Lintfort, Wesel, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen est né le 30 avril 1150, à Geldern, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, son père, Heinrich I. von Geldern, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Arnstein, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles avec Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. Il est décédé le 22 octobre 1207, à Zutphen, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Kamp-Lintfort, Kreis Wesel, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen am 30. April 1150 in Geldern, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heinrich I. von Geldern 32 und seine Mutter Agnes von Arnstein 31. Er hatte mit Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 22. Oktober 1207 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Zutphen, Geldern, Niederlande und wurde in Kamp-Lintfort, Kreis Wesel, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen è nato il il 30 aprile 1150 nel Geldern, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, il suo padre, Heinrich I. von Geldern, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Agnes von Arnstein, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie con Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. Morì il 22 ottobre 1207, nel Zutphen, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Kamp-Lintfort, Kreis Wesel, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphenが1150年4月30日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブジェルデルンで生まれた時，父Heinrich I. von Geldernは32歳，母Agnes von Arnsteinは31歳であった。彼はRichardis von Wittelsbach-Bayernとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1207年10月22日にオランダジェルダーランドズトフンで57歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス ウエセルカンプ-リントフォートにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen(이)가 1150년 4월 30일 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 클브 글더에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heinrich I. von Geldern(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes von Arnstein(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1207년 10월 22일 네덜란드 글델랜드 주트프에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 크레이스 웨셀 카p-린트포트에 묻혔다.

mn.30 April 1150–22 October 1207: 1150 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 30–1207 оны Аравдугаар сарын 22

pt.30 April 1150–22 October 1207: 30 de abril de 1150–22 de outubro de 1207
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen nasceu em 30 de abril de 1150, em Geldern, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich I. von Geldern, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Arnstein, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas com Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. Ele faleceu em 22 de outubro de 1207, em Zutphen, Gheldria, Países Baixos, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Kamp-Lintfort, Kreis Wesel, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen родился 30 апреля 1150 г. в местности джелдирн, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия, его отцу по имени Heinrich I. von Geldern было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Arnstein, 31. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери от Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. Он умер 22 октября 1207 г. в местности зутфен, джелдирланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности камп-линтфорт, крис весел, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

sm.1182–1207: mai le 1182 e o'o I le 1207

es.30 April 1150–22 October 1207: 30 de abril de 1150–22 de octubre de 1207
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Otto I. Graf von Geldern und Zutphen nació el 30 de abril de 1150, en Geldern, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, su papá, Heinrich I. von Geldern, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Agnes von Arnstein, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas con Richardis von Wittelsbach-Bayern. Murió el 22 de octubre de 1207, en Zutphen, Gelderland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Kamp-Lintfort, Kreis Wesel, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
